text: Hi, welcome to another episode of This is My Architecture. I'm Jhalak and we have Sean from ContactSuite today.
Hi Jhalak, it's great to be here.
Thanks Sean. Why don't we start with telling us about yourself and ContactSuite?
Yes, contact centres now more than ever need to be agile, flexible and scalable without the compromise of quality or agility.
Now, ContactSuite is a product which allows contact centres to scale. It enhances the agent experience but allowing both contact centres to both scale people as well as systems.
Right, I would love to see how you scale people. Let's walk straight into the architecture.
Alright, so we have our caller here. Caller Carrie needs some help from the contact centre.
She will make a call which is delivered into Amazon Connect. This will be chat or voice.
At the same time, Agent Andy, who has only just started with the contact centre today, logs in to ContactSuite via his browser using 2FA of course.

Caller Carrie comes into Amazon Connect. She might go into an IVR, interact with the IVR and then the call due to integration between Connect and ContactSuite directly, the call will be presented directly to Agent Andy using the same browser he's already logged into.
Right, so I'd love to see how you mentioned security here. How do you optimise time for Agent Andy?

Well, Agent Andy using some smarts automation and self-service. On the IVR, Carrie will input either touch tones or voice commands. Now, with logic through that and Connect contact flows and the use of Lambda, that will determine whether the call needs to be dealt with Agent Andy.

So, with Agent Andy, if it's slightly more complex or it can go through that self-service automated process. We have some microservices in the ECS platform which will connect out to some third party CRMs.
...of the likes of Siebel or Salesforce or so on. And then through, if they do need to go through Agent Andy, Agent Andy will receive a screen pop from ContactSuite.
This is a customised form which will have information, questions or processes that Andy has to follow. Alongside that, we have our SenseIQ knowledge base.

Now, SenseIQ can be embedded within that same ContactSuite screen pop or Agent Andy can have it on a separate browser instance. All this, all these tools together really help Agent Andy deal with Carrie's call on that first call.
Right. It is cool to see how Agent Andy is now enabled on his first day with knowledge base and self-services. You mentioned you have chat and voice. Do you get emails as well?

Yes, we do. We certainly do. Email is a big part of contact centres these days. We currently use SES with Amazon. Now, emails are handled in ContactSuite exactly the same as a chat or a voice. We can use the same automation using ECS microservices if they need to go to CRMs.
They are presented through to Agent Andy exactly the same with a screen pop.
Cool. So, chat, voice, emails all covered. Do you have two major statistics or KPI for contact centre?

Yes. So, KPIs and reporting are really crucial for contact centres. So, we retain data and we get the data directly from Connect, from ContactSuite itself and even SenseIQ. Now, these reports, these can be served directly into ContactSuite.
They can be sent out to customers or data can be sent out to customers or data can be put into tools using QuickSuite or the likes of Power BI.
Wow. It is great to see how contact centre is able to scale and provide better customer experience using AWS services.
Thank you. And we're really excited to see what AWS services are coming out next.
Thanks, Sean, for sharing this architecture. And thanks for watching. This is my architecture.
Thank you.
